rationale: Both correctly identify the ground truth root cause (default --run-level=core_model forcing dummy weights) and reach the same 'invalid' conclusion, citing akshatvishu/yenuo26. Y digs deeper with precise line-level citations across run_args.py, runtime.py, stage_config.py, and assertions.py, explains why the full_model pytest marker doesn't itself set run-level (a subtlety X misses), and addresses practical reporter concerns (video path, GPU memory split, hardware requirements) plus a constructive ergonomics suggestion. X's answer is correct and reasonably grounded but thinner on mechanism detail and wrapped in redundant report scaffolding rather than a clean maintainer reply.
